location.office_time_required: hybrid — combination of office and home working

level_signals: Leads and oversees programs with OEM customers from early-stage through production
Expected to establish comprehensive project management frameworks

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Extensive program management experience in complex automotive software development.
Proficient in utilising program management tools (e.g., Jira) to optimise workflow efficiency and ensure seamless project execution.
Robust technical expertise with comprehensive knowledge of the automotive industry.
Demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively across multidisciplinary teams.
Fluent Japanese and business-level English are required for collaboration with international teams and stakeholders

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Knowledge of current AI advancements, including Generative AI, AV 2.0, and Large Language Models (LLMs).
A Master's degree in Engineering or Computer Science.

scope.surface: OEM customer programs spanning advanced development to full-scale production (autonomous / assisted driving)
scope.business_model: B2B — AI software and foundation models sold to automakers (OEM customers)
primary_metrics_inferred: On-time delivery of customer program milestones
Customer satisfaction and first-level support responsiveness
Alignment of customer requirements with internal development pipeline
core_responsibilities: Lead and support cross-functional teams in executing complex customer programs, ensuring the successful achievement of program objectives.
Establish and maintain a comprehensive project management framework, including project planning, risk management, change management, and task coordination.
Engage with customers to understand and address their technical requirements and needs.
Collaborate closely with teams across Product, R&D, and other departments to translate customer demands into clear, actionable technical requirements.
Ensure efficient reporting both externally to customers and internally within the organisation.
Work closely with internal development teams to align customer requirements with internal milestone planning and execution.
Provide first-level support to customers, ensuring their needs are met promptly and effectively.

green_flags: Role has clear scope — customer-facing program management for OEM automotive programs
Hybrid working policy explicitly stated with core hours flexibility
Company explicitly encourages applicants who don't meet every requirement
red_flags: Fluent Japanese is a hard requirement — significantly narrows eligible candidate pool
Role location is Japan (Yokohama or Tokyo) but JD includes US-specific E-Verify and right-to-work references — possible copy-paste inconsistency worth clarifying
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
Posting date not available — recency of opening unclear

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'extensive' experience language and scope of responsibilities; no explicit title band or years stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description — AI software sold to automakers)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
ambiguities: The JD body states this is a Japan-based role (Yokohama or Tokyo) but the JD footer references US-specific documents (E-Verify Notice, Right to Work) typically included only for US postings. This may be a template copy-paste error or the role may have a dual-country component — worth confirming with recruiter.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship for Japan are not mentioned anywhere in the JD.
'Extensive program management experience' is not quantified in years — classified as hard due to unconditional placement under 'Essential'.
